source_ref: TheThing (handle 'Dimy'), 'Simple Daily System', ForexFactory thread 38981 (2007), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/38981-simple-daily-system — poster states he is not the author, only a follower; idea attributed to the forex-tsd.com community.
concept: Daily-bar trend continuation: three consecutive same-direction candles (by open/close vs prior day), optionally with 20>50 EMA and positive slope, trigger an entry on the next daily open with a two-step scale-out.
eligibility_reason: Fully mechanical daily FX rules (entry/stop/TP/scale-out all stated); pure MQL5, no ML, DXZ/.DWX FX daily data available; fixed-pip stop is FTMO/DXZ DD-compatible.
notes: Two variants of ONE core rule: (A) original requires 20/50 EMA(close) alignment + positive slope on both; (B) later 'NO Indicators' version (p7/p13) drops the EMA filter. Entry on OPEN of 4th daily candle after 3 consecutive days of Close>Open (long) / Close<Open (short). Candle qualification later clarified as higher open AND higher close vs prior day (not merely a bullish/bearish body). Stop = 2 pips beyond prior candle OR 90 pips, 'whichever is lower' (wording ambiguous; poster clarifies he uses ~80-90 pip fixed stop). TP1 30 pips (close half, move SL->BE), TP2 100 pips; per-pair TP tuning suggested (GBP/USD 39/80 or 20/60). Opens 2 lots. No time-based exit — only SL/TP closes. Ignore Sunday candle. Daily open = 00:00 GMT+2 broker time. JPY-cross tilt (GBP/JPY, CAD/JPY, EUR/JPY best in self-tests). Evidence = unverified 2007 self-reported paper trading; forum source lowers conviction; an in-thread member (Ebenst) noted <50% continuation after 3 consecutive daily bars.

source_ref: shovon (handle), 'Multiple & Modified Trading System', ForexFactory thread 390300 (forum discussion, undated), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/390300 — SMA-trend + engulfing/signal-candle swing system; idea credited to FF member '60 minuteman'.
concept: Enter a bullish/bearish engulfing (or a defined range-close signal candle) in the direction of a 10/20/50 SMA trend stack, sizing reward:risk to how strongly the SMAs align.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic SMA-trend + engulfing/range-close signal candle with defined SL and per-trend RR TP on FX majors/crosses (D1/H4); pure-MQL5, no ML, FTMO/DXZ-compatible.
notes: One strategy only; thread pages 5-7 and 9-11 duplicate 1-3 (pagination repeat); pages 4/8/12/13 are off-topic fundamentals chatter. Trend classes: overall = price vs 50 SMA; perfect = 10 SMA above/below both 20 & 50; temporary = 10 SMA between 20 & 50. Entry next-candle-open after signal candle; SL 1-2 pip beyond signal or prior candle extreme; TP keyed to trend (perfect 1:2, overall 1:1, temporary 2:1), 2% risk. Alternate signal candle precisely defined: close in top/bottom 25% of range AND its (or prior candle's) extreme = highest-high/lowest-low of last 5. Author excludes AUD/USD and commodities. PARTIAL: trend-direction filter stated as 'advised' not a hard rule, leaving one degree of freedom on which trend condition must hold to enter. Generic MA+engulfing pattern class (chart-pattern edges largely dead per FX-edge doctrine); low-reputability forum source, self-admitted inexperienced author, unverified '100% success' claim. Weekly/H4 are variants of the same core rules.

source_ref: MIH Bobby, 'EMA Fibonacci Level Trading Strategy', ForexFactory thread 416962 (14 posts, complete), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/416962-ema-fibonacci-level-trading-strategy
concept: Enter on the open of the next candle when price closes above/below (or bounces off) EMA(20) while RSI(10) crosses its 50 line and Stochastic(10,3,3) exits an OB/OS zone, taking profit at Fibonacci-offset levels (0.382/0.618) around the EMA.
eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 indicator confluence (EMA/RSI/Stoch) with stated SL/TP; FTMO/DXZ-compatible, but the 'trending vs sideway' regime filter and 'bounce back from EMA' entry are undefined.
notes: Generic MA+RSI+Stoch indicator-soup momentum combo with no structural edge and no correlated-driver fit (not event/energy/JPY/long-vol/session). Author's 'Fibonacci levels' are just 0.382/0.618 offset lines added to the EMA 'Level' tab. No backtest or trading results posted; thread replies (Davit, 4xplosion) themselves debunk OB/OS timing as lagging and suggest divergence instead. Author states M5/M15/H1 all viable (M15 chosen as central). TP variants: EMA-0.382 level or 0.618 level; SL 5-10 pips beyond EMA or 0.382 level for 1:2 RR. Low forum reputability.

source_ref: muhiuddin (handle), "Best Trading System (Only Momentum)" [MOMENTUM PRICE SYSTEM], ForexFactory thread 423512, forums.forexfactory.com, n.d. — https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/423512/best-trading-system-only-momentum
concept: Trade regular price/Momentum(28) divergence: enter on the close after momentum breaks its intra-divergence extreme (point F), betting the divergence resolves into a reversal.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic entry/exit/TP once divergence is fixed, pure-MQL5, stops + defined DD (FTMO/DXZ-compatible); but divergence-line identification is subjective and needs a mechanized swing/pivot heuristic.
notes: Rules: Momentum(28,close) on H4-D1. Bearish setup=price HH vs momentum LH; mark highest price as C, lowest momentum as F; short 2 lots on bar-close when F is penetrated. Bullish=mirror. SL at price C. TP1=|C-entry| (exit 1 lot, SL->BE), TP2=2xTP1 (trail 50% of profit each new candle), TP3=4xTP1 (trail 75%). Divergence swing lookback 8-28 bars. Author claims FX majors+crosses (incl JPY crosses: CADJPY/GBPJPY) + crude oil (XTIUSD), untested gold/silver; anecdotal forward-test only, no backtest, self-reported ~3.5-10%/mo with 32% DD, and multiple posters note different brokers show different momentum values -> divergence non-reproducible. Variant: pampaq applied same divergence idea to CCI14 on M15/M60 (same core, not a separate row). homealbum's EMA(21,8)+Bollinger 'price action' comment and KnSquare's Donchian breakout aside are too vague to be candidates. If built, the divergence detector (pivot-based regular-divergence, F=momentum extreme break confirmation) is the load-bearing mechanization risk.

source_ref: thomaas, 'Price Action made simple with Supply and Demand levels', ForexFactory thread 452780, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/452780-price-action-made-simple-with-supply-and-demand (author's free companion site www.21pips.com; method credited to Sam Seiden S&D videos and other FF threads)
concept: A consolidation 'base' preceding a strong impulse move is an unfilled supply/demand zone; a fresh first retest of that zone reverses price, entered with a limit order for a fixed risk:reward.
eligibility_reason: Core mechanic (impulse+base zone detection, fresh-only limit retest, SL beyond distal edge, fixed 1:2-1:3 TP) is deterministic and pure-MQL5 buildable, but the 'filter bad levels' and 'precisely draw levels' sections are [To come] and never delivered, so departure-strength / base-size thresholds must be parametrized by us.
notes: Zone types DBD/DBR/RBR/RBD; author says RBD and DBR are best. Filters: freshness (first touch only), strong departure (2-3 big same-colour candles), few candles in base. Entry at proximal edge, SL few pips beyond distal edge, TP fixed 1:2 or 1:3, risk 2.5%, pending orders, ~1-2 trades/wk. Primarily EUR/USD H1 but author claims any pair/TF and specifically notes gold (XAUUSD) works well -> testable on FX majors/crosses + XAUUSD. LOW priority: this is a classic support/resistance / S&D chart-pattern mean-reversion, the exact family the FX-edge doctrine (07-16) and Wyckoff/SMC closure (07-19/20) treat as structurally dead; brings no uncorrelated return driver. Variant in-thread (FerruFx #47): wait for a reversal pattern + momentum-break confirmation at the zone instead of a blind limit -> same core, fold into this row. redteamgo's discretionary crude/NG order-flow scaling is not mechanizable and not a separate candidate.

source_ref: 'The Dance Continues' (revival of 'The Dance' method by eclayf / 'Clay'), ForexFactory thread 460041, started by user '.Hawk.', c.2014 — https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/460041 (original concept thread t=236210). Rules taken from attached 'The Dance Manual.pdf' / 'The M15 Dance Manual.pdf' and .Hawk.'s in-thread trade posts.
strategy_name: The Dance — Two-Bar Touch Trade (M15 MA pullback continuation)
concept: In an established MA-stacked trend, enter with the trend when price races back in no more than two large bars to 'touch' a moving average (10 EMA, or the 35 SMA/50 EMA cluster) sloping at a steep angle, betting the pullback resumes the trend.
eligibility_reason: Core subset (3 MAs, trend stack = price+10 EMA on one side of 35/50, retrace-to-touch, fixed ~9-pip SL/TP or bar-extreme trail) is implementable in pure MQL5; but 'steep angle (~45deg)', 'race back' and 'large bar' are discretionary and must be proxied (chart-degree angle is scale-dependent -> normalized MA slope).
notes: Discretionary retail price-action method (originator eclayf/Clay); the 10 EMA touch and 35/50 touch are the same mechanic on different MAs -> one row. Trend def: price + 10 EMA above 35/50 (long) or below (short), with 35/50 tight/parallel = strong trend. Entry at the touch, NOT close-of-bar; SL tightened to ~9 pips then trailed, breakeven common; TP ~9-14 pips fixed (1:1 R:R). H1 confluence used as secondary filter; JPY variant (USDJPY) traded 19:30-21:00 GMT+2 Tokyo window. Screenshots only, no statistical evidence; author himself posts several -9 losses. FX-edge doctrine warns generic M15 MA-pullback rarely survives; kept ELIGIBLE per FF-source precedent (Balke), pipeline is the judge.

source_ref: 'The Dance Continues', ForexFactory thread 460041, user '.Hawk.', c.2014 — https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/460041 . Constrainment-trade description at p.19 ('3-4 small bars formed against 10 EMA restraining price ... constrainment trades off 10 EMA are weak, off 35 much stronger').
concept: Enter a trend continuation when a cluster of progressively smaller bars 'constrains' price against a moving average (weak off the 10 EMA, stronger against the 35 SMA) before price resumes the prevailing trend.
eligibility_reason: Concept (small-bar consolidation against a MA -> trend continuation) is mechanizable in principle, but this thread gives no precise trigger, entry level or exit for it beyond the shared ~9-pip stop model; underspecified here.
notes: Second of the method's two approaches ('pick one - constrainment or touch trades'). Far less specified than the touch trade in this thread; full definition lives in the linked 'Dance Manual' / 'Three Main Trades of The Dance' PDFs not present in this file. Shares the same 3-MA trend framework and exit model. Revisit / define precisely only if the touch-trade variant shows edge first.

source_ref: forexalien, 'Alien's Extraterrestrial Visual Systems', ForexFactory thread 463573, 2017, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/463573 (PDF captures opening posts, forum pages 1-3)
concept: Discretionary visual read of RSIOMA (>50 long, <50 short, >80/<20 strong trend) aligned across D1-to-M1, confirmed by a 5-speed ADX and an RSIOMA-vs-delayed-stochastic 'rubber band' separation, to ride momentum trends.
eligibility_reason: Purely discretionary visual method: no numeric entry trigger, no exit/stop/take-profit or sizing rules stated; depends on proprietary custom indicators (RSIOMA variant with extra oscillator, 'D Delay Stochastic.ex4') with unknown internals.
notes: Author self-describes as 'a Visual type of trader'; entries are eyeballed ('look for a place to start thinking about shorting'), exits are 'ride the trend to the end' / discretionary monitoring. Components mentioned but never fully specified: (1) RSIOMA 50/80/20 level system, (2) multi-timeframe wave alignment D1-M1, (3) 5-speed ADX 7/21/42/89/144 all-up/all-down trend gate, (4) RSIOMA+delayed-stochastic separation setup, (5) price-vs-RSIOMA divergence for reversals. Edge claims are marketing ('98% accuracy', hindsight chart examples) with no evidence trail. Would revisit only if OWNER supplies a fully mechanized rule set and the custom indicator sources; as posted it is not implementable.

source_ref: txfxtrader (originator), with indicators by Groenie ('Hitman') and Nonor ('dashboardRSI55'), 'MTF RSI Trading System', ForexFactory thread #504229, 2014. https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/504229
concept: Take the RSI of the last closed bar on each selected timeframe (M1-D1); go long only when RSI is above 50 on ALL of them and short only when below 50 on all, i.e. a multi-timeframe momentum-alignment filter with fixed TP/SL.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic MTF RSI-vs-50 alignment with fixed SL/TP; pure-indicator, no ML, FX data available via .DWX, and FTMO/DXZ-compatible once the optional progressive MM is dropped in favor of RISK_FIXED.
notes: Forum retail system; edge evidence purely anecdotal (~10%/month ROI claim, author admits no backtesting, disclaimer 'what works today may not work tomorrow'). Signal uses the PREVIOUS closed bar per timeframe and the threshold is the 50 line (not classic 30/70). Variants collapse to one core rule set: RSI period 2 or 3 (Groenie 'Hitman' indi) or 55 (Nonor dashboard); TP/SL 30/20 pips or later 60/30; D1 optional, H4 added mid-thread as a noise filter. Pair screen: spread < 4 pips (later <3) and 5-day ADR > 60 pips. Optional flat-close at end of traded session. A progressive/martingale MM (0.1,0.1,0.2,0.4,0.8; cap 5 entries, ~2% cumulative) was proposed then EXPLICITLY REMOVED by the author to avoid confusion, so the core carries per-trade stops and is not a stopless martingale. Trigger timeframe is under-specified (lowest aligned bar); M15 chosen as a representative execution TF for the MTF confluence.

source_ref: StingrayEA, 'Bollinger Band & Stochastic', ForexFactory thread 506226 (27 posts, 2 pages), retail forex forum, ~2019 (thread references Bitcoin trading and 2019-era handles), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/506226-bollinger-band-stochastic
concept: Price crossing a Bollinger band confirmed by a Stochastic main/signal cross and a reversal candlestick triggers a directional entry.
eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 indicator combo (BB 20/2, Stoch 14/3/3), deterministic, FX H1 .DWX data available, fixed SL 50 / TP 50 / trailing 15 compatible with FTMO/DXZ.
notes: Generic retail BB+Stochastic combo with no equity curve or backtest evidence; multiple skeptics in-thread (dkrock: stochastic wrong tool, price 'walks the band'; billbss: 'I don't see many winners'). OP's stated breakout framing ('price crosses upper band from below = breakout') conflicts with the reversion logic in Magix's posted MQL4 snippet (fade the band tag on a reversal candle, Stoch overbought/oversold) — one core idea, two directional interpretations. 'Bullish/bearish engulfing' is the reversal-candle filter. Yet-another indicator-swing variant, not an uncorrelated driver.

source_ref: TampaDave (reply in StingrayEA's thread), 'Bollinger Band & Stochastic', ForexFactory thread 506226, retail forex forum, ~2019, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/506226-bollinger-band-stochastic
concept: Enter when price sits outside a Bollinger band and Stochastic crosses back from an extreme (below 20 up / above 80 down), scalping a fixed ~10-pip mean-reversion move.
eligibility_reason: Implementable in pure MQL5 once the Stochastic cross is mechanized and a fixed/ATR SL is chosen; FX M15/M30 .DWX data available; but no stop-loss is ever specified.
notes: Discretionary self-report on a live 0.01-lot account (2 weeks), no logs/backtest. Fixed 10-pip target criticized as non-portable across pairs (reteid2222), who suggests ATR-based SL and SMA20 / EMA100-150 exits; 'decisive crossover' is subjective (author warns against entering on a mere touch). Same indicators as Row 1 but clear mean-reversion logic vs Row 1's breakout framing, hence a separate row; author also mentions trading Bitcoin (crypto, out of universe) and M30 as an alternative TF.

source_ref: "1 Hour after daily open", author Nik13, ForexFactory thread #535657 (~2014, not explicitly dated in the excerpt). https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/535657/1-hour-after-daily-open
concept: After the daily (Sydney) open, the close of the first H1 candle relative to the daily-open line signals directional continuation through the low-volatility pre-London session.
eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 deterministic daily-open/first-hour-close breakout with fixed 10-pip SL/TP on EURUSD/GBPUSD; .DWX data available, has stops, FTMO/DXZ-compatible.
notes: Base rules: buy if first H1 closes above the daily-open line, sell if below; SL=TP=10 pips; EURUSD+GBPUSD opened together with a basket equity-TP (close both when combined +10 pips). Symmetric 1:1 with very tight stops - author concedes SL likely hit >75% and member Mariodrugs reports a 2-week test as 'not worth'; edge doubtful but cheap to test as a session-structure driver. Optional martingale MM overlay (pages 12-13) is OUT OF SCOPE / incompatible with FTMO+DXZ. imran.78p's discretionary variant (DO +-15-20 entry, opposite-side stop, MA100/200 hourly filter, hold for the day) is VAGUE/manual and not carried as a separate row.

source_ref: "1 Hour after daily open", author Nik13, ForexFactory thread #535657 (~2014, not explicitly dated in the excerpt); 'Another similar system' post. https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/535657/1-hour-after-daily-open
concept: Fade the first post-open hour by placing a pending stop at the daily-open line opposite to the first H1 candle's direction, betting price re-crosses the open.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic pending-stop re-cross of the daily-open line on FX majors; implementable in pure MQL5 with stops, but TP not firmly specified by the author.
notes: Nik13's 'another similar system' (page 13): if the first H1 candle is bearish place a BUY-STOP at the daily-open line, if bullish place a SELL-STOP; SL 10, TP ~10 ('but not sure, may be more'). Distinct edge from Row 1 (re-cross/fade vs continuation), hence a separate row. No forward/backtest evidence provided in the thread.

source_ref: Kosomolate, "No long story - RSI system", ForexFactory thread 539300, 2015, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/539300-no-long-story-rsi-system
concept: Counter-trend mean-reversion buying when a very short RSI(2) drops below 30 and selling when it rises above 70, expecting a snap-back from the overbought/oversold extreme.
eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 RSI(2) OBOS with explicit stop (SL 25) and TP 10 + break-even mgmt; deterministic, EURUSD M15 data available, FTMO/DXZ-compatible. But OP's headline backtests (1k->68k over 10y) were not reproducible by two forum members.
notes: RSI period 2, upper 70 / lower 30; entry buy RSI<30 / sell RSI>70; SL 25, TP 10, break-even trigger 5 with BE+ 1 (pips, MT4 EA). Symbol unstated by OP but backtested/discussed on EURUSD M15. Reputability: OP posted only equity-curve images (600% claims), MathTrader7 could not replicate with the attached EA and argues a single unfiltered OBOS signal has no structural edge (RSI in trend = 'power zones', not reversal). Classic Connors-style RSI(2) MR variant — yet another OBOS mean-reversion, not an uncorrelated driver; low edge plausibility despite complete rules.

source_ref: abokwaik, 'ATR Break Out' (ABO Trading System), ForexFactory thread 562470 (year n.d.), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/562470/atr-break-out
concept: Momentum breakout: at each new bar place buy-stop/sell-stop pending orders an ATR-multiple away from price, so a move beyond average bar size triggers a trend-following trade managed with ATR-scaled SL/TP/trailing.
eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 ATR straddle breakout with ATR-based SL/TP/trailing stop and risk-% sizing; deterministic; FX-majors + Gold H1 data available via .DWX; each order carries its own stop (not martingale/grid).
notes: One core rule set. Entry: at start of each new bar, place pending stop orders at BreakOut_ATR_mult x ATR(period, default 50) from price (straddle both sides; optional 'Follow Last Bar Direction' filters to one side); untriggered pendings deleted and re-placed each bar until filled. Exits: SL = SL_ATR_mult, TP = TP_ATR_mult, plus ATR trailing stop. Params stated: BO mult 2-3, SL 2-4, TP 4-20, TS 6, ATR period 50; optional MACD+RSI filters (suggested by auvergnat78) to cut false signals. Variants = same core: 'default' (BO x3 + RSI on) vs abokwaik 'crazy'/aggressive (BO x2, filters off, MaxOrders 99, multiple simultaneous orders). Also run on XAUUSD M15, GBP/USD M30, USD/JPY, USD/CAD (instrument-agnostic momentum). Trading-Time window configurable (author uses European session for EURUSD). Caveats: forum-sourced (reputability); user Kubiasty reported persistent losses, author admits Gold-M15 gives many false signals in ranges and default settings 'fire mostly at red-news times' (event-vol angle worth isolating); MaxOrders up to 99 / multi-order stacking = concentration risk, implement with capped max orders. MT4 .ex4 originals only (rebuild in V5 MQL5).

source_ref: Sis.yphus, "A Proven Simple Strategy (2MAs, 1 RSI)", ForexFactory thread 574065, ~2016 (tests Jan 2010-Dec 2015). https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/574065/a-proven-simple-strategy-2mas-1-rsi
concept: Short-term counter-move entry within a trend: when a 2-period RSI hits an extreme and price pulls back to the fast 5 MA while staying on the 200 MA's trend side, fade the pullback and exit when price closes back across the 5 MA.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic MA/RSI mean-reversion on FX majors daily; pure MQL5, no ML, single fixed-lot position, low reported per-pair DD (~6%), FTMO/DXZ-compatible; classic Connors RSI-2 variant.
notes: Long: close>200MA AND close<5MA AND RSI(2)<5. Short: close<200MA AND close>5MA AND RSI(2)>95. Exit at close of candle that touches/crosses 5MA (Option 1) or immediately on touch (Option 2). No hard SL/TP by design (author found none improved it); 5MA exit is the risk cap, framework overlay adds DD guards. Tested EU/GU/AU/NU/UJ/UCHF/UCAD, avg PF 1.31 / avg maxDD 6.19% in-sample (NZDUSD losing at PF 0.95). VARIANTS discussed in thread (same core rules): H1 (dafa: best TF for EU), M15/M5 intraday tests; RSI thresholds 30/70 instead of 5/95 (BillYon/dafa); optional fixed SL + trailing-stop exit (Shemski/abokwaik) gave lower DD on H1. TwoMA_OneRSI.ex4 posted by abokwaik as reference. Reputability: retail forum, in-sample only, modest edge (PF ~1.1-1.3), a swing/short-term-MR FX variant of which the farm likely holds several -> MED not HIGH.

source_ref: swilliam (opener), "TMT Scalping System" (Trend-Momentum-Trendline), ForexFactory thread 577639, 2018 (indicator zip uploaded Jul 22, 2018); https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/577639/tmt-scalping-system . Original system credited by the opener to BabyPips Free Trading Systems (secondary source, not linked).
concept: Trade a trendline break out of an EMA pullback in the direction of the daily-open bias and EMA(7)/EMA(20) trend, confirmed by RSI and MFI momentum filters.
eligibility_reason: Defining entry trigger (hand-drawn trendline from swing + break) and exits are explicitly discretionary per the thread ('no EA draws the trendlines correctly', 'exit depends on trader'); RSI/MFI filter thresholds are unstated custom-indicator colors, so no deterministic rule set exists as described.
notes: Reconstructable-but-new variant: dropping the trendline and using a mechanical close-back-through-EMA(7) trigger inside a daily-open + EMA(7)>EMA(20) trend, with RSI>50 / MFI>50 as momentum gates and fixed SL 10-20p / RR 1:1-1:3, is a generic FX trend-pullback scalper (Sam68 argues the trendline is secondary and the MA-cross + daily-candle + two momentum 'escalators' are the core) — but that is a substituted, under-specified system, not the one stated. Cost drag severe at 10-20 pip targets on FX. Timeframe variants M1/M5/M15 mentioned; some posters (toppo qqq, Hops) applied it to DAX30/GER30 with wider 35-40 pip SL and traded without SL. Forum source, no track-record evidence beyond screenshot claims. Revisit only if a distinct mechanical breakout definition and indicator thresholds are chosen — treat as a new candidate, not this thread's system.

source_ref: ahmedabbas, 'Simple RSI & EMA high Profitable ratio Strategy', ForexFactory thread 599061 (2016), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/599061-simple-rsi-ema-high-profitable-ratio-strategy — origin attributed to TEB63's earlier FF thread 229238 (http://www.forexfactory.com/showthread.php?t=229238), the 'Bagovino Method', and a Thomas Carter Amazon ebook.
concept: Trend-momentum: enter on an EMA5/EMA12 crossover confirmed by RSI(21) and CCI(80) on the same side of their midlines, exit on the opposite cross or an oscillator flip through 50.
eligibility_reason: Fully mechanical EMA-cross + RSI/CCI momentum filter, implementable in pure MQL5, FX majors on H1/H4 available via .DWX, hard SL present (FTMO/DXZ-compatible).
notes: Generic MA-cross + oscillator trend filter, no structural/uncorrelated edge (redundant with existing index/FX swing variants). Entry: EMA5 crosses EMA12 up AND RSI(21)&CCI(80)>50 (long) / down AND both<50 (short), enter on candle close. Exit: EMA5 recrosses EMA12 OR RSI&CCI flip 50. SL ~35-60 pips by pair volatility (discretionary but bounded); reference MQL4 logic posted by trenki2. Thread's OWN backtests falsify it: trenki2 EURUSD H1 2001-2016 'fails badly' with default (5,12,21)/30-pip SL, only curve-fit (136,195,191)/36-pip 'better'; xuantianfeng negative; TEB63 'I never seen an EA work with this system'. Discretionary variants folded in (not separate rows): TEB63 London-open-to-2h-before-close + candle-pattern entry + higher-TF RSI confirm + 1:3 RRR (VAGUE); emmanuel7788 multi-TF RSI-50 bias (W/D/H4 aligned) + ADR-100 stops + 50-pip-min trailing, exit on RSI-M15 reverse (PARTIAL/discretionary). Low-reputability forum source; keep only as a cheap Q02/Q04 sacrificial-crossover baseline.

source_ref: "Trading Strategy Bladerunner", posted by ruben-trader (copy-paste of Erron's article, AuthenticFX), ForexFactory thread 604020, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/604020 ; original source authenticfx.com free-forex-... (Bladerunner strategy)
concept: Trend continuation: use the 20 EMA (or Bollinger mid-band) as a polarity line and enter on a successful retest/rejection of the EMA in the direction price is currently respecting.
eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 codeable (20 EMA + candlestick logic), deterministic SL (2 pips beyond signal candle) and 1R/2R TP with breakeven trail; FX M5 data available; FTMO/DXZ-safe (fixed stops, no grid). Core mechanic testable but relies on discretionary overlays (breakout-of-consolidation precondition, S/R + round-number + fundamentals confluence, 'convincing' close).
notes: Generic 20-EMA pullback/retest trend-continuation, one of countless similar; author explicitly warns no entry on naked EMA rejection alone (needs confluence/fundamentals we cannot mechanize) -> low mechanical-edge plausibility, no unique return driver. Variants (ONE row): (1) single order vs two split orders (TP1=1R closes half, remainder to breakeven), (2) 20 EMA vs Bollinger 20 mid-band vs combined 'forex polarity indicator' ribbon. Buy/sell-stop 2 pips beyond confirmatory candle, orders expire at next candle open. Built-in news filter: no entry 30-45 min before scheduled news, wait 15 min after. Forum content is an acknowledged copy-paste of Erron/AuthenticFX -> reputability caveat, lowers priority. Pages 6-7 are only thread chatter.

source_ref: Trading System and Strategy Review, thread by mrhigurashi (with TheJube, thorn, profitfarmer, kraven), ForexFactory, thread 612863, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/612863-trading-system-and-strategy-review
concept: A discussion thread inviting unbiased reviews of other traders' systems; it contains opinions and pointers to other threads but no self-contained tradable rule set.
eligibility_reason: Meta/review discussion with no concrete entry/exit/parameter definitions; only references to external threads (a 'fuzzy' trend-pullback system explicitly stated to have no exit strategy, and thread t=590623 described only in generalities) plus generic HH/HL pullback commentary.
notes: Points to other FF threads (post 9224087 trend-pullback system; thread 590623 'simple entry/exit + position management, fixed SL, no martingale') that would be the actual sources if harvested separately, but no rules are reproduced here. kraven's HH/HL break-then-pullback remark is too underspecified to implement.

source_ref: Masterrmind, "Quad Candle System" (QCS), ForexFactory thread 668957, ~2017-2022, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/668957-quad-candle-system
strategy_name: Quad Candle D1 Reversal at Murrey-Math Extremes
concept: On D1, a 2-up-then-2-down candle sequence (short) or 2-down-then-2-up (long) at an extreme Murrey-Math octave level triggers a mean-reversion entry expected to roll into a longer trend.
eligibility_reason: Mechanizable kernel: deterministic D1 candle-color sequence + public Murrey-Math (period 64) extreme-zone filter, ATR/octave-gap SL (1-2 OG beyond cluster), RR-based TP to opposite MML, exit on 2 opposite-color candles or trailing; FX D1 data available, stops present, Friday/swap handled by framework.
notes: Forum source (Balke precedent = not auto-reject) but reputability weak: author is selling a withheld 'Advanced Traders Toolkit'. The claimed edge lives in NON-mechanizable overlays (Gann squaring/SQ9, planetary/astro aspects, moon-phase yearly-reset, harmonics, discretionary fundamental bias, correlated-pair confluence) which must be dropped; author himself concedes '2up-2down has been around as long as Japanese candles' and 'I make no ownership of this method in its pure form'. 'Master Octive Trading Frame' is disclosed later in-thread to be Murrey Math Lines (0/8,8/8,+/-1/8,+/-2/8 zones), so the level filter IS public/deterministic. Additional-entries scheme is capped-risk scale-in pyramid (each leg has its own SL, sum of SLs < total assigned risk 1.5-15%), NOT a stopless martingale. Same core as the 'V Method'/'V trading system' (Paul Langham) referenced in-thread. Generic D1 swing-MR variant on FX majors/crosses; extreme-MML filter lowers frequency.

source_ref: ForexFactory Thread 707474 'Channel MA Short-Term System', author handle MickeyMar, forexfactory.com forum, 2017 (indicator/EA attachments dated Oct-Nov 2017), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/707474
concept: MA-crossover trend-following that filters range-bound whipsaws by requiring the 33 EMA(close) signal line to cross fully beyond a channel formed by 55 EMA(high) and 55 EMA(low).
eligibility_reason: Deterministic EMA-channel crossover, pure MQL5, hard 40-50pip SL (capped 50), FX majors on .DWX, Friday/session close available; FTMO/DXZ compatible.
notes: Channel = 55 EMA(high) + 55 EMA(low); signal = 33 EMA(close). Long when signal crosses ABOVE 55EMA(high), short when below 55EMA(low); signal valid only on CLOSED M15 bar (author insists candle-2/candle-1). Two entries: (1) Normal = immediate market entry when price near channel; (2) Delayed = when crossover fires >40pips from channel, place pending order at 33EMA level, fills on pullback to signal MA. Stop: hard 40-50pip, or reverse at opposite signal. Exit variants: fixed 40pip TP w/ BE at +20-25; flexible 210pip cap closed at end of session (author's preferred, usually NY close); or no-TP close-at-opposite-signal. Options author wants in EA: first-signal-only vs take-all-reconfirmations; optional H1-cross confirmation filter; optional Asia hour-filter for non-JPY/AUD/NZD/USD pairs. Favored pairs USDJPY/EURUSD/GBPUSD; intended as 15-20 pair correlation-managed basket. ~20 trades/mo per pair (~8-9 losers) -> well above Q02 freq floor. CAVEATS: forum source, author admits discretion in live trading and offers no backtest/track record; pure technical trend-following with no structural limit-to-arbitrage basis (FX edge doctrine) -> expect Q04/Q05 scrutiny. Minor same-core variants (NOT separate rows): diamonddbw 3-pip-retrace-to-33 entry w/ 20-25pip SL; forexbandit7 5M-retrenchment pending entry; Lifestudent3 Bollinger-band pullback idea (unadopted/vague).

source_ref: Simple Daily Trend Reversal Trading System, ForexFactory thread 713593, opened by user 'mrdfx' (2018), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/713593-simple-daily-trend-reversal-trading-system (Part II: thread 864690)
concept: Trend-reversal entry taken only when a (repainting) ZigZag arrow, an MBFX/Turbo-JSRX stochastic overbought-or-oversold reading, a custom trend indicator, and a candle closing back across a 10/15-period MA at a Fibonacci pivot zone all agree.

notes: Mainly H4 (also H1/D1), FX majors + GOLD. Exit is discretionary (fixed 50-100 pips / Fib / %ADR / opposite signal / trailing, plus manual 'monitor price action'). Variant by user 'ebeckers' adds a S/R-level filter and a channel-surfer filter and a H1-confirms-H4 multi-TF rule. Page 3: the mechanized SDTR EA was 'discontinued temporarily. Due to the poor results... removed.' Mechanizable skeleton (stochastic exit-of-OB/OS + trend filter + MA close-cross near Fib pivot) is generic and overlaps yet-another swing/reversal-MR variant; would require inventing all indicator definitions and a mechanical exit.

source_ref: ForexFactory thread 771822, "Statistics combined with system. Profitable? What do you think", started by user michaellobry (c.2018), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/771822-statistics-combined-with-system-profitable-what-do-you
concept: Enter each bar in the direction implied by the previous candle's wick asymmetry (buy when lower wick exceeds upper wick = low rejection, sell otherwise) with symmetric fixed TP/SL.
eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 deterministic candlestick rule with fixed 50-pip SL/TP; EURUSD data available; not martingale/grid (fixed lot, hard stop).
notes: Author claims M1-D1; example uses H1. Base rule fires every bar; thread proposes many optional filter params (reverse/anti-trade switch, only-trade-N-candles-after-swing HH/HL/LH/LL, prev-candle >X pips, lower-wick >X pips, lower-wick >150% of upper-wick, prev-candle >200% of prior 4 candles) -- treat as one parametrized EA (variants, not separate strategies). Edge is a candle-wick direction bet; contradicts FX chart-pattern-dead doctrine, hence LOW. Every-bar entry raises Q02 economics + pending-stacking concerns. Community backtests (Fauzaan, lassame) inconclusive; one user repurposed the .ex4 for rebate churning (not a strategy).

source_ref: AR(1) autoregressive trading EA by user MathTrader7, reposted in ForexFactory thread 771822 (michaellobry); original ForexFactory thread 562689 (https://www.forexfactory.com/showthread.php?t=562689); this PDF https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/771822-statistics-combined-with-system-profitable-what-do-you
concept: At each daily open, detrend the last N closes, fit an AR(1) model, confirm stationarity via a Dickey-Fuller test, then go long/short (stop-and-reverse) based on the model's one-step-ahead prediction versus the current detrended value.
eligibility_reason: Classical econometrics (AR(1) OLS/Yule-Walker + Dickey-Fuller test), not ML libraries -- implementable in pure MQL5 arithmetic; EURUSD D1 data available.
notes: Distinct statistical return driver vs the wick bet. Lookback N unspecified; SL/TP left as an open question (base exit is reversal-only) -- needs a hard stop for FTMO daily-loss/max-DD. Author reports a 2000-2015 EURUSD backtest via attached .ex4 (not verifiable here). Thread also floats ARMA/ARIMA/GARCH extensions -- no non-ML barrier but higher curve-fit risk; tzamo explicitly warns against over-optimization.

source_ref: Neural-network MACD pattern system by user vladi1979 in ForexFactory thread 771822 (michaellobry), uses R + h2o deep-learning framework bridged to MT4; https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/771822-statistics-combined-with-system-profitable-what-do-you
concept: Train a deep neural network on MACD-indicator patterns across 28 pairs (JPY scaled by /100) to predict the next price change and automate entries/exits.
eligibility_reason: Deep neural network + external R/h2o runtime -- violates the V5 no-ML hard rule and the no-external-runtime constraint; rules only sketched (author defers to a personal website).
notes: Timeframe unstated in thread. Revisit trigger: only if reduced to a fully-specified non-ML linear/arithmetic proxy -- under V5's permanent no-ML hard rule the NN form itself is never eligible. Demo-only (148 trades over 3 weeks), unverifiable.

source_ref: GazFx, "Trend Continuation Strategy", ForexFactory thread 837301 (~2018), method attributed to Sam Seiden (CME floor trader). https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/837301-trend-continuation-strategy
concept: Enter in the EMA50 trend direction on a Stochastic(5,3,3) crossover out of the oversold/overbought zone, targeting continuation of an established trend at a fixed 1:3 risk:reward.
eligibility_reason: Fully mechanical Stochastic+EMA rule set with explicit SL/TP/trailing; pure-MQL5 implementable, deterministic, data available for FX/metals; forum/indicator-marketing source lowers priority but does not reject.
notes: Buy: Stochastic(5,3,3,Close/Close) cross-up at/below 20 while EMA50 slopes up; Sell: cross-down at/above 80 while EMA50 slopes down; ignore when EMA50 flat. SL just below prev low (buy) / above prev high (sell) + 10-pip buffer on H4; TP = 3x SL; trailing stop = entry stop value. Author claims ~60% win / 2.45 PF but unverified and tied to a paid 'Market Scanner' MQL5 indicator upsell. 'Any TF / any symbol' claimed; H4 stated as best, H1 also tested. Scanner covers 28 FX pairs + Gold + Silver; one replier reported it working on DAX (Indices) -> also testable on Metals/Indices. Generic retail Stochastic+EMA pullback = low edge plausibility, hence LOW despite FULL rules.

source_ref: The DIBS Method (Daily Inside Bar Setup), method by trader 'PeterCrowns', compiled/continued by jarroo in ForexFactory thread 86766 'The DIBS Method... No Free Lunch continues' (continuation of thread 56907 'No Free Lunch but all the coffee you can drink'), 2008, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/86766-the-dibs-method-no-free-lunch-continues
concept: Anchor a daily reference at 06:00 GMT (00:00 GMT for JPY pairs); on an up-day (price above the open) buy the break of an hourly inside bar, on a down-day sell the break, riding intraday continuation with a stop on the far side of the IB.
eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 deterministic breakout: IB detection, daily-open direction filter, entry 1 pip beyond IB range +spread, initial stop at opposite IB extreme, exit half at 1:1 (FTT) then hold remainder as free trade; FX-H1 data on .DWX; bounded risk fits FTMO/DXZ.
notes: Well-specified core rules; open-hour (06:00 GMT majors vs 00:00 GMT JPY) and remainder-trailing method (fixed initial stop / MA-trail) are sweepable params, not gaps. Session-structure/breakout driver, orthogonal to index/metal swing-MR. Best trades stated in first 6-9 hours after the open (London into early NY). Optional 'hot hand' relative-strength filter (trade the currency most up/down on the day) and M15/M30 variants mentioned but not the core. Forum-sourced, but detailed and rule-based (Balke EA precedent from ForexFactory). An MQ4 identifier 'DIBS_TriggerLines' was posted in-thread as reference only.

source_ref: Fx-ken, 'Simple Horizontal Line Trading H1', ForexFactory thread 922813, 2019, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/922813 (secondary contributor jarfx05). Uses 'Tro's Sweet Spots Gold' round-number indicator set to 25.
strategy_name: Round-Number Pending Breakout with SMA50 Trend Filter
concept: Pending stop orders placed 3 pip beyond 25-pip round-number levels (000/250/500/750), taken only in the direction of the SMA50 trend, aiming to ride round-number breakouts/momentum on FX crosses.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic and pure-MQL5: SMA50 direction filter + 25-pip round-number grid + pending stop 3 pip past the level + fixed SL 30 / TP 50 pip (RR 1:1.5) + BE+1/5 trail after 10-15 pip; GBPUSD/EURJPY on .DWX; fixed SL is FTMO/DXZ-compatible. Forum source with only anecdotal manual-backtest evidence (20 trades, 55% WR one month) caps priority.
notes: Core pair set GBPUSD + EURJPY (EURJPY = JPY-cross driver); OP tilts to Asia-to-London hours but explicitly says trade anytime, so no hard session gate. 'Don't enter when the round line is too close to the MA50' needs a chosen distance threshold. Sideway regime: switch to TP 25 / SL 25 (RR 1:1). Variants to fold into one test family: (a) H4/D1 using 100-pip round lines with RR 100/200 or 50/100 (pages 14, 25); (b) MA200 instead of MA50, OP claims higher win rate (page 27); (c) EMA vs SMA interchangeable. Secondary contributor jarfx05 runs the same 00/25/50/75 round-number idea on Renko candles ('Candle Point O', 5-12.5 pip bricks) plus pivot-break entries (WS1) — same concept but Renko-based and under-specified (VAGUE); Renko is awkward for the MT5 tester, so treat as a PARKED-flavored variant, not a separate testable candidate.

source_ref: foff00, "4x25MA Simple Strategy," Forex Factory thread 932507 (year not stated in thread; MT4-era), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/932507
concept: Go long only when price closes above the 25 EMA on all four timeframes (M15, H1, H4, D1) simultaneously, riding confirmed multi-timeframe trend agreement with ATR-scaled stop and target.
eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 deterministic MTF 25-EMA trend with ATR(14) SL=2xATR / TP=3-4xATR on FX majors and JPY crosses; all data via .DWX, FTMO/DXZ-compatible with a defined stop.
notes: Entry: price above 25 EMA on M15+H1+H4+D1 with 1-3 closed bars (shorts mirror). Exit: SL 2xATR, TP 3-4xATR (ATR timeframe unstated, presumably H4). Session filter: enter only after London open, not after NY close (London-through-NY window). Author later tested an M15 scalper variant with trailing-stop-only (original had NO SL) - anecdotal forward-test evidence only, no rigorous backtest. Secondary reference in-thread: reteid2222 'Strategy I: 3 EMAs with trend filter' links external site forexmt4indicators.com plus NRTR_ATR_STOP.ex4 - rules not stated in thread, not classified. Much of thread (pages 8-17) is off-topic drama about a 'MACD Sample' .ex4 flagged as malware/HTML payload - ignore; not a strategy. source_ref: "Magic 100" EMA-100 trend-continuation pullback system, thread started by user Minotawr (with rule clarifications by xkwisit2001), ForexFactory thread 989445, first 3 pages / 60 posts, n.d. (approx. 2021-2023). https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/989445-magic-100
concept: Trend-continuation: in the direction of a 100EMA-defined trend, place a pending stop order just past the last counter-trend retracement candle (SL at that candle's opposite extreme, TP=2R), rolling the pending order to each new retrace candle until triggered.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic pure-MQL5 pending-stop breakout with EMA100 trend filter, hard SL at candle extreme and 2R TP (FTMO/DXZ-compatible); FX-major H1 data available via .DWX.
notes: Core mechanic well-specified (EMA100 direction; buy-stop above / sell-stop below the retracement candle 'just the spread away'; SL at the same candle's low/high; TP 2R at ~1:2-1:3; roll the pending order to each newer retrace candle on close until filled; 1 trade/day/pair, major pairs, Frankfurt+London session). PARTIAL because the load-bearing 'strong trend vs sideways = don't trade' filter and 'which retracement candle' are discretionary/unquantified (mechanizable via EMA100 slope + first-opposite-color-close). Generic EMA-pullback archetype; anonymous beginner-oriented FF thread with no backtest evidence -> low priority. Variants mentioned: HTF confirmation (4H/D1 trend, 1H entry) and applying on D1/H4 directly; treated as parameterizations of the same core.

source_ref: LauraT, 'Roadmap - A Way To Read Markets', ForexFactory thread 993524 (2020), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/993524-roadmap-a-way-to-read-markets
concept: When an 8-EMA high/low channel sits fully above (below) the SMA200 the bias is long (short), and you enter on price pulling back to the opposite channel band, filtered by a composite multi-timeframe RSI>50/<50 and an SMA200 multi-timeframe dashboard, exiting at prior-day high/low or the daily-open line.
eligibility_reason: Entry skeleton (channel-vs-SMA200 bias + pullback-to-band + RSI/MTF filters) is mechanizable in pure MQL5 with available data, but stops/exits are only heuristic and the author states human judgement is essential and her own automation attempts failed to replicate performance.
notes: Discretionary indicator suite, not an algo; OP explicitly says she tried twice with skilled coders and could not automate it. Two named setups fold into this one core: (1) 'channel trade' = pullback entry after channel fully crosses SMA200; (2) 'channel failure' = channel pokes past SMA200 then falls back, treated as higher-probability reversal entry (same execution: wait for channel on one side, enter when price reaches opposite band). Instrument/TF-agnostic in the thread: she trades M1 for indices (Dow) and oil, M5/M15 for FX crosses (GBPUSD, EURAUD), also H4/D1; representative M15/FX chosen for the row. Filters: composite RSI = RSI2/RSI9 blended across W1/D1/H4 (>50 bullish); MTF dashboard wants >=2 relevant-TF SMA200 readings agreeing. Exits vague: prior-day High/Low, daily-open line (broker-midnight, CET), or discretionary 'take profit on spikes'; no fixed SL stated. Advanced ADR-locked reversal element mentioned but deferred with no rules. Generic MA-channel trend-pullback with no uncorrelated return driver -> low value; exits would need framework redesign (cf. T-WIN/Balke 'edge real, exits amputate' pattern).

source_ref: clemmo17, 'The Finance Book Club', ForexFactory thread 993609, 2019-2020, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/993609/the-finance-book-club
concept: A running review of ~30 trading books plus Coursera (Shiller 'Financial Markets') and Tickmill (Munnelly) course notes; it argues trading philosophy and critiques technical analysis rather than specifying any complete mechanical system.
eligibility_reason: No source in the thread presents a complete deterministic entry+exit+param system; it is book reviews, course notes and TA-skepticism. The closest concrete content is generic textbook TA the summarizer explicitly dismisses.
notes: In-depth sections: Mark Tier '23 habits', Shiller Coursera notes (portfolio/CAPM/behavioral finance), Taleb 'Fooled by Randomness', Munnelly Tickmill course, Aronson 'Evidence-Based TA'. The Aronson review's own conclusion is that ~6400 data-mined binary S&P500 buy/sell rules fail significance testing (upward bias) - an anti-strategy result. Munnelly's only quasi-rules: Bollinger-band 'fade' of the outer band in a low-volatility London session (mean reversion) and a pullback-to-MA trend continuation in high vol - both stated without parameters/vol thresholds and dismissed by clemmo as 'traditional TA garbage'. Munnelly money-management scheme (80-pip stop, 160-pip TP, scale out 2/3, trail final third +80 pips) is position management, not an entry edge. Books with reputed concrete systems (Alan Hull 'Trade My Way', Naked Forex 'Big Shadow', Owens&Lizotte, Jeff Cooper 'Hit and Run', Trader Vic 2B) appear only in the graded table of contents with no rule write-up. Nothing implementable harvested.

source_ref: sharpiemomo, "Simple System", ForexFactory thread 1184099, year n.d. (archived 2026-07-24), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1184099-simple-system
strategy_name: APB Heiken-Ashi Color-Flip NY-Open Reversal Scalp (US30)
concept: Enter on the first opposite-color APB (modified Heiken-Ashi) bar close during the NY open on US30, stopping at the prior momentum extreme, to capitalize on price rejections at tops/bottoms.
eligibility_reason: Entry+stop are mechanical (first opposite modified-HA close, stop at prior swing extreme, US30 NY 09:30-12:00 EST) and testable on WS30 M5/M15 .DWX data; exit is fully discretionary ('no TP rule', fast partials ~0.5RR, scratch runners) and must be mechanized (opposite-flip or session-flat) before backtest.
notes: APB = modified Heiken-Ashi indicator (APB.mq4/.ex4 attached, MT4). Single discretionary scalp system, not multiple strategies. Author frames it as only a 'marginal edge' relying on discretionary 'Risk Per Click' sizing (1-4 clicks, non-%; replace with RISK_FIXED). Range-bar variant is not testable in the time-based tester (optional, ignore). Rejected member's TP variant (APB flip touching 5 EMA). Fits session-structure driver rather than swing-MR, but plain HA color-flip is a weak mechanical prior; LOW due to forum source + discretionary exit/sizing core.

source_ref: Voldemar227, 'VR Gap Open Source Trading Strategy', ForexFactory forum thread #1394867, 2026, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1394867-vr-gap-open-source-trading-strategy (open-source EA also published on MQL5 Code Base: MT4 https://www.mql5.com/en/code/9994, MT5 https://www.mql5.com/en/code/72239).
concept: On each new bar, if the gap between the prior bar's close and the current bar's open exceeds a threshold, fade the impulse betting the price returns to close the gap.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic gap-fade fully specified (entry threshold, direction, fixed-point SL, TP=gap size); pure MQL5, no ML, has hard stops so FTMO/DXZ-compatible.
notes: Entry: at new-bar open, if |prev_close - cur_open| > MinGapSize -> Buy on downward gap, Sell on upward gap; market entry, fixed lot (use RISK_FIXED for backtest). Exit: SL = fixed points from entry; TP dynamic = the gap size itself (target = the level that fully closes the gap). Params: Trading Volume, Minimum Gap Size, Stop Loss Size, Magic/Slippage; no indicators/trend/news filters. Source says any TF/instrument, but real gaps only occur at weekend/session boundaries on continuous .DWX feeds -> best tested as a weekend/session-gap fader (Monday open; index cash gaps); intraday bar-to-bar gaps ~0 on 24h FX. Forum post is essentially an open-source-EA advert -> moderate reputability. Gaps above threshold are rare -> watch Q02 frequency floor (>=5 trades/yr).

source_ref: Hugh Briss, 'Swing trading with heiken ashi and stochs', ForexFactory thread #340556 (posts #1, #20; HA-doji refinement post #22 p8), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/340556-swing-trading-with-heiken-ashi-and-stochs (anonymous forum author; no verified statistics)
concept: Trade in the direction of the 100-SMA trend and enter on a Heiken-Ashi colour flip back into the trend confirmed by a Stochastic (8,3,3) cross out of the pullback extreme.
eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 (Heiken Ashi, 100 SMA, Stochastic 8,3,3) trend-pullback with fixed 50-pip stop and defined MM exits; deterministic, FX-cross data available via .DWX/DXZ, FTMO/DXZ-compatible.
notes: One core strategy with three MM/exit variants (ONE row): (1) 50-pip SL, hold until HA flips against trend, trail stop behind price; (2) 50-pip SL + 50-pip TP, SL->+1 after +25 pips; (3) 50-pip SL, take half at +25, remainder to breakeven + trail. Entry rule: price vs 100 SMA = trend filter (long only above / short only below); wait for pullback (HA colour change against trend), HA colour flips back into trend AND stoch cross near the extreme, enter on 4H candle close. Refinement (p8, post #22): HA-doji reversal entry (small body, long wicks both ends after a smooth counter-trend pullback). Discretionary currency-strength filter (post #20) to pick among simultaneous signals; CAD/JPY-vs-oil intermarket musing (post #39-42) is a one-off observation, not mechanical. Anonymous forum author, no verified stats; qualitative 'smooth cross/pullback' language must be mechanized (stoch oversold/overbought thresholds, HA colour-change detection). Covers JPY crosses (e.g. cadjpy). Crowded FX trend-follow archetype -> MED.

source_ref: muhiuddin, "Best Trading System (Only Momentum)" / MOMENTUM PRICE SYSTEM, ForexFactory forum thread #423512 (n.d., archived 2026-07-24), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/423512-best-trading-system-only-momentum
concept: Reversal at momentum exhaustion: enter against price when the Momentum(28) indicator diverges from price and then breaks its own prior swing extreme (point F).
eligibility_reason: Momentum divergence + momentum-swing-break trigger, price-based stop (point C) and measured-move TPs are mechanizable in pure MQL5 (pivot-lookback divergence detection); no ML/external runtime; FX/JPY/crude data available; defined stops = FTMO/DXZ compatible.
notes: Rules: Momentum period 28 (close), divergence drawn over 8-28 bars; SHORT = price higher-high while momentum lower-high (mark highest price C, lowest momentum F); enter on close when F penetrated with 2 lots; SL at price C; TP1 = |C - entry| (exit 1 lot, SL->BE); TP2 = 2xTP1 (trail 50% of profit); TP3 = 4xTP1; LONG is the mirror. Divergence-line identification is subjective and must be parameterized (pivot lookback) to be deterministic -> PARTIAL. Evidence is forum anecdote only: author self-reports 3.5-10%/month with 32% DD over 11 months, forward-tested, no backtest; one member reported very few H4 signals. Author claims it works on all FX pairs incl. JPY crosses (CADJPY/GBPJPY examples) and crude oil (XTIUSD in our universe), untested on gold/silver; H4-D1 swing (avg hold ~4 days). Variants mentioned in thread (same core concept, not separate rows): CCI14 divergence on M15/H1 (PAMPAQ #51), and homealbum's vague EMA(21,8)+Bollinger 'price action' comment (#56) too vague to build. A community MT4 divergence indicator (Momentum Divergence2_mtf+alerts.mq4) was attached, confirming the divergence detection is automatable.

source_ref: petefader, "1,500 PIPS PER MONTH with this method, VSA/SR/fibb etc", babypips.com forums, 2009 (posts #9 and #136). https://forums.babypips.com/t/1-500-pips-per-month-with-this-method-vsa-sr-fibb-etc/21084
strategy_name: Volume-Spike Wick Rejection at S/R-Fib Level
concept: Fade a resistance or 50-61.8% fib level when price hits it and prints a candle with a >=40% rejection wick on a tick-volume spike, signaling smart-money absorption/exhaustion.
eligibility_reason: Entry is a deterministic MQL5 primitive (tick volume available; wick-% + volume-vs-recent-range + proximity to level), no ML/external feed; but exits and the level definition are unspecified and must be operationalized.
notes: Rule stated twice verbatim: (1) move up to .50-.618 fib or resistance; (2) M5/M15 candle with >=40% wick on top + volume spike vs recent range; (3) enter short on reversal; mirror for longs. Variants: M15 anchor, M1 for finer scalp entry. Source is a 2009 discretionary trader repeatedly accused of running a paid signal room with zero posted track record; VSA-on-forex tick volume is disputed within the thread itself, and no SL/TP system is defined (trade examples use ad-hoc 20-pip SL / ~40-pip targets / lock-at-BE). Other ideas floated in-thread are too vague for rows: an undisclosed '1hr strategy, 90% win ratio' (never revealed), a range-breakout-via-volume-asymmetry read, and an MA5/MA-cross pullback (pete states he does not trade a strict crossover). Could rise to MED if reframed as a session-open exhaustion fade on JPY crosses (he traded GBPJPY/EURJPY/USDJPY across Asia->London->NY) with a defined ATR/fixed-pip stop.

source_ref: Thread "10 pips / day (NBoP)", author handle PIPology (MT4 indicator-setup help by Sweet_Pip), babypips.com Forex forum, 2008, https://forums.babypips.com/t/10-pips-day-nbop/10624 (full ruleset in posts #11 and #13; %R-vs-RSI-cross filter refined in posts #66/#100).
concept: On an 8-EMA(close)/13-EMA(open) cross confirmed by a MACD(13,21,8) zero-line cross within 4 bars, enter on a Williams %R(8) pullback (below -20 long / above -80 short) that is NOT crossing RSI(13,open), taking a fixed 10-pip target against a 10-pip+spread stop.
eligibility_reason: Mechanical core (EMA cross + MACD zero-cross within 4 bars + %R pullback + %R-not-crossing-RSI filter + fixed 10-pip TP + 10-pip/+spread SL + ~15-30min time exit + no-Friday) is fully specified and buildable in pure MQL5 on FX majors/JPY-crosses; only the hand-drawn trendline / previous-day linear-regression-channel breakout confirmation is discretionary and would be dropped or approximated as a fixed linreg-channel break.
notes: Original chart TF is 30M (nearest allowed enum = M15); trades resolve in 3-15 min which is finer than the signal TF (execution-granularity red flag). Fixed 1:1 R:R 10-pip scalp with spread eating the target = marginal net economics on spread-inclusive .DWX feed; classic indicator-confluence FX scalp with no structural limit-to-arbitrage edge (contra FX-Edge-Doctrine). Promotional context (blog/YouTube/feed, refuses template + account statement) and winning trades posted a day later (hindsight bias, called out in posts #115/#91) lower confidence but do not by themselves reject per forum-source rule. Pairs traded: EUR/USD, GBP/USD, USD/JPY, CAD/JPY (avoids AUD/USD, GBP/CHF, divergence days, news). Variant suggested by user enochbenjamin (#75): scale out at 10 pips, trail remainder at break-even. An undisclosed H4 'long trades' system is referenced (#77) but never specified -> not a candidate.

source_ref: harmonicphil et al., "3 Little Pigs Trading System", BabyPips forum thread, 2013, https://forums.babypips.com/t/3-little-pigs-trading-system/54174 — strategy originates from the free 'Forex Useful' (Jon) e-book/manual; SL detail p.1-2, ATR-symmetric TP EA variant p.13/17.
strategy_name: 3 Little Pigs Multi-TF SMA Trend Swing
concept: Trade in the direction of three aligned timeframes: enter only when price is on the same side of the 55-SMA (Weekly), 21-SMA (Daily) and 34-SMA (H4).
eligibility_reason: Fully specified deterministic multi-TF SMA trend system on FX majors/crosses; implementable in pure MQL5, no ML, all data on .DWX (W1/D1/H4).
notes: Entry: buy when Close>SMA55(W1) AND Close>SMA21(D1) AND an H4 candle touches then closes above SMA34(H4) (enter on that close); sell vice versa. SL = SMA34(H4) offset by 25%*(ATR14_high+ATR14_low) pips; trail behind SMA34. Pairs: AUDUSD,EURGBP,EURJPY,EURUSD,GBPUSD,USDCAD,USDCHF,USDJPY; risk 1%/trade; positions held over weekend. Exit variants (same core rules => one row): (a) OP author = trailing-stop-only, open target; (b) nicolas74 EA = symmetric ATR take-profit TP=Close+/-25%(ATRmin+ATRmax)+/-(Close-SMA34) over last 30 H4 bars; (c) kashif = fixed SL/TP. Caveats: generic triple-MA trend follower, choppy-market weak; multiple thread EA-builders reported inconsistent/negative results and that trailing-SMA stop and fixed SL both underperform, and one MT4 backtest had SL-not-hit coding bug (unreliable evidence). A paid MT4 confirmation indicator exists but is NOT required — all logic is stated. 'PAST' system name-dropped (p.2) but never specified.

source_ref: Eliteforexpartner, 'A Scalping/Day Trading strategy 5minute timeframe', forums.babypips.com, thread #1266726, 2024, https://forums.babypips.com/t/a-scalping-day-trading-strategy-5minute-timeframe/1266726
concept: A MACD zero-line cross sets trade direction ('campaign'); after price pulls back into a Bollinger Band, a buy/sell stop 1 pip beyond the pre-pullback swing extreme catches the momentum-continuation breakout.
eligibility_reason: Mechanical M5 breakout-after-pullback: MACD(6,17,1) zero-cross direction filter, Bollinger(10,shift 1,dev 0.66) pullback, stop order 1 pip past marked swing high/low, defined SL/TP variants (1:1 or 2:1) - pure MQL5, deterministic, defined stops, DXZ/FTMO-compatible.

Generic trend-continuation breakout, no distinctive uncorrelated driver. Three exit methods: M1 SL 1 pip below breakout candle at 1:1 RR, M2 SL 1 pip below lower band at 2:1 RR (both mechanical), M3 discretionary trend exit (wedge break / third-touch overshoot) - excluded as non-deterministic. 'Draw resistance at pre-pullback high' = swing/pivot-high detection, mechanizable. Wick-above-high without close = re-mark high (repeat step 3). Cancel unfilled stop order on opposite zero-line cross. Author claims it also works on indices and precious metals.

source_ref: pipcompounder et al., "(ALMOST) Perfect Swing Trading using Trendlines", BabyPips forum thread, 2012-2013, https://forums.babypips.com/t/almost-perfect-swing-trading-using-trendlines/48649

eligibility_reason: Discretionary hand-drawn-trendline core is non-deterministic, but OP gives an explicit mechanical proxy (SMA 8/17 close crossover on H4) and medisoft gives a linear-regression/standard-deviation-channel breakout variant; both are pure-MQL5, deterministic, no ML, use 100-pip stops, and GBPNZD/NZD-cross data exists via DXZ/.DWX.

Marshalmax variant = line between last two fractals with angle filter (fractals repaint -> determinism caveat). Risk: SL ~100 pips, TP let run (500-600 pips typical) or 100-120 pip trailing stop, move to BE after ~70-100 pips. Correlated pairs cited EURNZD/GBPAUD/EURAUD -> basket potential. 'TrendMeLeaveMe' MT4 EA trades a manually named trendline object (not auto-mechanizable). Forum anecdote only, no backtest evidence; GBPNZD high spread / exotic cross cost; NZD-news volatility repeatedly noted as move driver. Priority lifted above REJECT by trend-following FX-cross diversification vs the MR-heavy index/metal portfolio; held down by commoditized MA-cross mechanization and zero evidence.

source_ref: currency_tunes (original poster), thread 'Ichimoku Trading System', BabyPips forum (forums.babypips.com), 2008-2009, https://forums.babypips.com/t/ichimoku-trading-system/18242 (mechanized exit/stop variant 'Ichimoku 2' by user ArmyDoc, 2009, posts #121-#123)

Generic FX trend-following, no structural/limit-to-arbitrage edge argument -> low priority per FX-edge doctrine. source_ref: unhommefou, posts #25-#39 in thread 'Ichimoku Trading System', BabyPips forum (forums.babypips.com), 2009, https://forums.babypips.com/t/ichimoku-trading-system/18242

source_ref: London Breakout Strategy - 1H Timeframe Modified, by Cloudninee (Cloud Nguyen), BabyPips.com Forums, 2020, https://forums.babypips.com/t/london-breakout-strategy-1h-timeframe-modified/326993
concept: Trade the London-open volatility expansion by entering on the H1 close that breaks the high/low of the last 3 Asian-session candles, SL at the opposite range end, fixed 1.5 R:R, forced flat before NY close.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic session ORB, pure-MQL5, fixed SL/opposite-range-end + 1.5 R:R TP + EOD-flat; FX majors/crosses all on .DWX; FTMO/DXZ-compatible (stops + Friday-close exist).
notes: Core rules (p1): range = H/L incl. wicks of last 3 H1 Asian candles before London open; enter at CLOSE of first candle that breaks AND closes clearly outside range (no pending orders, avoids fakeouts); SL = entry-to-opposite-range-end (p29 #68), TP = 1.5x SL, close before NY/US close if neither hit (p1); lot = risk/SLpips/10 (p42 #119); risk 1-2% (later 1%). Symbols: started EURGBP/EURUSD/GBPJPY/GBPUSD/USDCHF/USDJPY, dropped the 2 USD pairs -> kept EURUSD,EURGBP,GBPUSD,GBPJPY. tommor (#28): select by ATR spike at London open (~2x daily ATR low) -> GBP/USD strongest, EUR/USD next, USD/JPY has no spike -> avoid. Independent backtests ~42-50% WR (izner 42% #102, soundscape8 40-50% #108, thebrettski2 ~50/50 #99); TradeOrBeASlaveForever (#32) got negative results since 2013 -> edge marginal/crowded, spread+commission matter. VARIANTS (same core, one row): SamDavies EA uses Tokyo-close->NY-open range window + entry offsets + auto-close 6pm GMT+1 (his GBPJPY Martingale sub-variant is REJECTED-incompatible); thebrettski2 adds Aroon Up/Down 67-line filter (#99); range-def variants (#90) = whole Asian session / last Asian candle / first London candle; also a 15m-TF version. Discretionary bits (skip large-Asian-range days, mid-range SL on strong breakout, skip NFP/news days per #107/#115) must be fixed as deterministic params. Same range-breakout family as live Balke but intraday-flat (EOD close) vs Balke overnight hold -> fits session-structure intraday-flat sourcing direction. Forum source: lower priority for reputability but precedent (Balke) allows it.

source_ref: "My Hedged Healing Circle (Positive Swap)" by PeaceKeeper (with masrois46), Babypips Forums, 2019, https://forums.babypips.com/t/my-hedged-healing-circle-positive-swap/207133
concept: Open a ring of mutually-hedging FX/gold positions (weighted by margin ratio) that net to near-zero direction but collect a positive aggregate broker swap, then hold until accrued carry drags the basket to positive equity ('hit and run' at ~0.5-1%).
eligibility_reason: Edge is 100% broker-specific positive swap (injected as $0 in our tester, non-reproducible, hard rule against invented swap) with no price signal, no stops, and intentional hold-to-recovery drawdown of 100-200% of margin — incompatible with FTMO daily-loss / DXZ 5%/20% DD gates; author himself states it is 'not backtestable'.
notes: Multiple ring variants are the same core carry-basket idea (one row): (1) 12-pair XXXUSD+XXXCHF+XXXJPY safe-haven ring 1-1-1 or 0.5-1-1; (2) 6-pair FX Major USD ring; (3) 10-pair FX Minor ring; (4) XAUUSD-SELL/XAUEUR-BUY gold carry pair (XAUEUR not in .DWX universe anyway); (5) EURCHF/EURJPY swap-arb pair. No deterministic entry timing (just 'open the ring'), exits discretionary. Author admits post #1 that positions are often net-directional ('you are simply buying the dollar'), confirmed by poster #3. Swap values quoted differ per broker (EURJPY, AUDUSD, GBPJPY flip sign) — not modelable.

source_ref: NickB's 4H Scalping Method (method by Nick Bencino / 'NickB', forex4noobs.com), thread started by user phil838, Babypips forum, 2009. https://forums.babypips.com/t/nickbs-4h-scalping-method/23747
concept: Trade the break of a horizontal 'scalp line' drawn at a prior swing high/low that produced a large, sustained reversal, betting resurgent order-flow momentum as price revisits and breaks that level.
eligibility_reason: Fixed 50-pip SL/TP + pending-order break with 5-7 pip buffer is fully mechanical; the 'scalp line' qualification (swing pivot with >=~350-400 pip move over >=4-6 candles, line-age expiry) is mechanizable as a pivot/retracement filter, but participants stress it is discretionary ('a thinking man's system, not mechanical').
notes: Core rules: GBP/JPY H4, no indicators; entry = pending stop order on break of scalp line with 5-7 pip buffer (= broker spread) to filter fake breaks; SL 50 / TP 50 pips (1R), some let winners run to 100+; scalp-line validity 2 months (strong) / 1 month (weak), single-use (invalid once traded through); only trade after London open (needs momentum; Asian low-vol breaks skipped); ebook wants V/U/L-shaped bounces. Variants in same core (ONE row): manual S&R line-break (needs price-spike confirmation, no pending order), 'master candle' break, and reversal-off-line all trade price interaction at horizontal levels. Extends to USD/CAD, EUR/JPY, NZD/JPY, EUR/USD per posters. CAVEATS: forum source, unverified 70-80% win-rate marketing claims; overlaps shop doctrine that discretionary S&R/chart-pattern breakouts have shown no durable mechanical FX edge (cf. Balke range-breakout: edge real but died Q05 DD). Mild fit as JPY-cross + London-session-structure driver keeps it ELIGIBLE for a mechanical-proxy test rather than a clone.

source_ref: KGMK, 'Premarket Range Break + 10:30 Confirmation Strategy', Babypips Forums, 2025, https://forums.babypips.com/t/premarket-range-break-10-30-confirmation-strategy/1282095
concept: On a gap-up stock, buy the post-open breakout of the premarket high, then require a second breakout of the 10:30 high while holding above VWAP as confirmation to filter fakeouts.
eligibility_reason: Single-name US cash-equity gap play (premarket session, 9:30 open, VWAP, earnings/news-driven gaps) with no individual-stock data in our .DWX/DXZ universe; rules are one anecdotal example with no exit/target and subjective 'consolidates', too vague to mechanize.
notes: Low-reputability single forum post (one post, handle KGMK, 2025). Only example given: premarket high 102, break at 9:45 holds above VWAP, 10:35 break of 10:30 high 103 -> entry 103.10, stop 102.40 (below VWAP); no take-profit rule stated. Generic opening-range-break + confirmation concept already covered by existing index/gold ORB lineage (e.g. 20007 GOLD_BREAKOUT, Balke range-break) and is single-stock-specific, so no new uncorrelated driver.

source_ref: KGMK, "Range Between Tokoyo/London on 1m [33% Combined Profit, 70% Winning Rate, Majors]", BabyPips forum, 2025, https://forums.babypips.com/t/range-between-tokoyo-london-on-1m-33-combined-profit-70-winning-rate-majors/1282228

eligibility_reason: Results-dump only: the four entry/exit 'Rules' are referenced by number but never defined; no methodology, thresholds, or parameters stated, so the strategy cannot be implemented as written.
notes: Concept (session-structure range on M1 majors during Tokyo/London window) fits our favored uncorrelated-driver bucket, but is un-harvestable as posted.
